blocks.content: Third period ended and the hallway filled up fast.

Daniel was on the other side. Not close enough for anything to happen. He looked up at exactly the right moment — the way people do when they've already been watching — and for about two seconds we were just across the hall from each other. Then he looked away.
Daniel passed me on his way to class. Didn't say anything. Didn't have to. The message had already been sent.

I saw Daniel at the water fountain on the other side. He clocked me. Then the corner of his mouth moved — not quite a smile, but something close. Like he'd put something in a drawer he could come back to.
Daniel came around the corner. He stopped half a step when he saw me — like he was confirming something. Then kept walking without a word.
Whatever this was, it wasn't finished.
